label: Wycliffe's Bible
description: John Wycliffe's followers complete the first full English translation of the Bible.

label: Jan Hus burned at the stake
description: Reformer Jan Hus is executed for heresy at the Council of Constance.

label: Gutenberg's printing press
description: Johannes Gutenberg develops the movable-type printing press in Mainz, enabling mass Bible production.

label: Luther's 95 Theses
description: Martin Luther posts his 95 Theses in Wittenberg, sparking the Protestant Reformation.

label: Tyndale's New Testament
description: William Tyndale's English New Testament is printed, the first from Greek into English.

label: Calvin's Institutes
description: John Calvin publishes the first edition of the Institutes of the Christian Religion.

label: Geneva Bible
description: The Geneva Bible, widely used by English Puritans, is first published.

label: King James Bible
description: The Authorized (King James) Version of the Bible is published in England.

label: Mayflower lands at Plymouth
description: English Puritan separatists land in Massachusetts aboard the Mayflower.

label: Peace of Westphalia
description: The Thirty Years' War ends, reshaping religious and political boundaries in Europe.

label: First Great Awakening begins
description: A wave of evangelical revival begins in the American colonies and Britain.

label: French Revolution begins
description: The French Revolution begins, reshaping Europe's political and religious landscape.

label: Modern missionary movement begins
description: William Carey founds the Baptist Missionary Society, widely seen as the start of the modern Protestant missionary movement.

label: British transatlantic slave trade abolished
description: Britain's Parliament passes the Slave Trade Act, ending the transatlantic slave trade in the British Empire.

label: Slavery abolished in the British Empire
description: The Slavery Abolition Act ends slavery across most of the British Empire.

label: Darwin's On the Origin of Species
description: Charles Darwin publishes On the Origin of Species, prompting major theological debate.

label: American Civil War begins
description: The United States descends into civil war, fought in part over slavery.

label: Slavery abolished in the United States
description: The Thirteenth Amendment abolishes slavery throughout the United States.

label: Boxer Rebellion
description: Anti-foreign, anti-Christian uprising in China; many Chinese Christians and missionaries are killed.

label: Edinburgh Missionary Conference
description: A landmark world missionary conference held in Edinburgh, shaping 20th-century missions.

label: World War I begins
description: The First World War begins in Europe, lasting until 1918.

label: Russian Revolution
description: The Russian Revolution brings the Bolsheviks to power, leading to decades of persecution of Christians in the USSR.

label: Wall Street Crash
description: The Wall Street Crash triggers the Great Depression.

label: World War II begins
description: The Second World War begins, lasting until 1945.

label: Communist revolution in China
description: The Chinese Communist Party takes power under Mao Zedong, leading to severe persecution of the church.

label: State of Israel established
description: The modern State of Israel is founded.

label: Fall of the Berlin Wall
description: The Berlin Wall falls, symbolizing the end of the Cold War division of Europe.

label: Soviet Union dissolves
description: The USSR dissolves, ending decades of state-enforced atheism across Eastern Europe and Central Asia.
